title: Is Shinji moe Yes No Please keep it on topic
display_title: Is Shinji moe Yes No Please keep it on topic
wikitext:
[]: Is Shinji moe: Yes/No? (Please keep it on topic.)             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Mar 30, 2011 12:38 pm Somewhat of a very loose crosspost with this thread . Also, a note: Please keep this thread on topic. That means discussing Shinji's gender ("...I'm a boy.") and/or sexuality (Straight? Bisexual? Questioning? Simply a 14 year old boy who doesn't even know what he wants from others let alone himself? Who knows!) is okay as long as it's within reason and doesn't overpower the thread. So.... Let's discuss whether or not Shinji is moe, shall we? Considering fans tend to think Asuka and Rei are moe, male moe IS possible and all. Similar to male tsundere (see also: Kyon from Haruhi ). And IMO, so far Sadamoto's manga!Shinji has somewhat of a male tsundere personality. Somewhat. (New Movie Edition Shinji, OTOH, is somewhat of an anime and manga hybrid, but it's still too early to give a complete call on his personality since we're only halfway through.) Image Source: http://i74.photobucket.com/albums/i267/sailorstardust/Fandom/16_20090614010507.jpg Several months back I stumbled across this blog which makes some good points, but to elaborate... Shinji still strikes me as more of [s]mai waifu[/s] a waifu than a husbando because of his personality, but damnit that's neither here nor there. He's cute (I pray he'll take after Yui's "pretty" looks--instead of young!Gendo's bug-eyed looks and instead of Pengel's/fan-artists "willowy librarian" look--in the post-3rd Impact world.) , he can cook, he can clean, he can play the cello, he's very polite and usually somewhat sweet/good-natured. That is, unless you fuck with him/push him over the edge in some manner... Then, you're screwed. See also: Shami battle, Zer battle, Asuka in EoE and EoE in general.... Butttttttttttt, when it comes to his personal issues (daddy and mommy issues/Oedipus Complex, low self-esteem issues, his Depression/Anxiety, etc), doesn't it make you want to give him a great big hug and tell him everything will be alright, EGF? And no, this isn't just the Shinji fangirl in me talking, I really do find the kid moe .

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed Mar 30, 2011 12:47 pm Considering the way the fanbase treats him I'd say he's moe. And I'd also agree that he needs a hug more then anything else. Beating down on him like some people do certainly wouldn't help matters.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Mar 30, 2011 12:54 pm

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Wed Mar 30, 2011 1:00 pm Yes.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Mar 30, 2011 1:01 pm TheEvaMonkey : Oh, you. (Nabbed it from my photobucket, eh? Wonder what the original source is anyway.) Does that mean you agree Shinji's indeed moe or you just like this thread? I'm interested in your thoughts. :) child of Lilith : Fanbase "treats him" how? You mean usually feeling bad for him, or something else...? It does seem a great deal of EGFers feel bad for Shinji as opposed to the fans that feel he needs to "man up" or "stop being a pussy" ( Missing the point of his character and the times he does "act manly"!), but...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Mar 30, 2011 1:08 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: ...you just like this thread?

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Wed Mar 30, 2011 1:11 pm Yes, he is a male version of moe.

You mean usually feeling bad for him, or something else...? It does seem a great deal of EGFers feel bad for Shinji as opposed to the fans that feel he needs to "man up" or "stop being a pussy" ( Missing the point of his character and the times he does "act manly"!), but...

Does that mean you agree Shinji's indeed moe or you just like this thread? I'm interested in your thoughts. :) child of Lilith : Fanbase "treats him" how? You mean usually feeling bad for him, or something else...? It does seem a great deal of EGFers feel bad for Shinji as opposed to the fans that feel he needs to "man up" or "stop being a pussy" ( Missing the point of his character and the times he does "act manly"!), but...

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Wed Mar 30, 2011 2:18 pm Nadia = Shinji. This was pulled right out of Yoshiyuki's ass.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Wed Mar 30, 2011 2:33 pm Is water wet? Do bears poop in forests? Is pope a ... okay, let's not go there. ...on the other hand, if moe is defined strictly by audience reactions to the character, then he might not be... or it's a subjective thing. But then, if that's the case and Shinji's not a moe, then no male character ever will be.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Wed Mar 30, 2011 2:33 pm ^ You don´t need to post the same old thing twice, you now.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Wed Mar 30, 2011 2:57 pm For all intents and purposes, yeah, he does fit the criteria. I'm going to go put a shotgun in my mouth now.

[]:             Quote Post by Light » Wed Mar 30, 2011 3:44 pm I meant no as in Shinji is not moe. Mods are removing posts? Derp.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Wed Mar 30, 2011 4:15 pm Xard wrote: duuuuuuuuuuuude wat

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Mar 30, 2011 4:17 pm Allemann wrote: I belong to a group of anime fans who consider calling something moe disparaging. Serves him right, the twerp.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Wed Mar 30, 2011 4:22 pm Xard wrote: As for me, Shinji is mostly pitiful

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Mar 30, 2011 4:29 pm Hey. guys -- not wanting to sound all NO FUN in here, or wanting to have every post made be deathless prose, but when the back and forth thins out to IRC level, then that's one of the reasons we have an IRC channel. @topic No -- Shinji doesn't make me feel any moe whatsoever.
